words.def: a statement that something is true although it has not been proved and other people may not agree with or believe it
words.example: claim about somebody/something, The company had made false claims about its products.

words.def: relating to classical music
words.example: a classical composer/musician/pianist

words.def: easy to understand and not confusing
words.example: She gave me clear and precise directions.

words.def: the act of pressing the button on a computer mouse or touchpad
words.example: a mouse click

words.def: the regular pattern of weather conditions of a particular place
words.example: a tropical/warm/mild/temperate/cold climate

words.def: an act of climbing up a mountain, rock or large number of steps; a period of time spent climbing
words.example: an exhausting climb

words.def: material made by weaving or knitting cotton, wool, silk, etc.
words.example: woollen/cotton/linen cloth

words.def: an object, a piece of evidence or some information that helps the police solve a crime
words.example: The burglar left no clues.

words.def: a person who trains a person or team in sport
words.example: the head/assistant coach

words.def: a hard black mineral that is found below the ground and burnt to produce heat
words.example: I put more coal on the fire.

words.def: a small flat piece of metal used as money
words.example: gold coins

words.def: a group of objects, often of the same sort, that have been collected
words.example: He wanted to share his vast art collection with the world.

words.def: having a particular colour or different colours
words.example: brightly coloured balloons

words.def: to come together to form a single thing or group; to join two or more things or groups together to form a single one
words.example: combine to do something, Hydrogen and oxygen combine to form water.

words.def: a play, film or TV show that is intended to be funny, usually with a happy ending; plays, films and TV shows of this type
words.example: a romantic comedy

words.def: something that you say or write that gives an opinion on or explains somebody/something
words.example: comment on something, She made helpful comments on my work.

words.def: connected with the buying and selling of goods and services
words.example: residential and commercial properties

words.def: to do something wrong or illegal
words.example: to commit a crime/an offence

words.def: the activity or process of expressing ideas and feelings or of giving people information
words.example: communication between A and B, Good communication between team leaders and members is essential.

words.def: the process of comparing two or more people or things
words.example: For Durkheim, comparison was the most important method of analysis in sociology.

words.def: to take part in a contest or game
words.example: The games were an outstanding success, with almost 2 400 athletes competing.

words.def: used to describe a situation in which people or organizations compete against each other
words.example: competitive games/sports

words.def: a person who takes part in a competition
words.example: Over 200 competitors entered the race.

words.def: a reason for not being satisfied; a statement that somebody makes saying that they are not satisfied
words.example: a formal complaint

words.def: in every way possible; in every part
words.example: It was completely different from how I had imagined it.

words.def: to give all your attention to something and not think about anything else
words.example: I can't concentrate with all that noise going on.

words.def: to decide or believe something as a result of what you have heard or seen
words.example: conclude something, It is too early for us to conclude anything.

words.def: something that you decide when you have thought about all the information connected with the situation
words.example: We can safely draw some conclusions from our discussion.

words.def: feeling sure about your own ability to do things and be successful
words.example: She was in a relaxed, confident mood.

words.def: to state or show that something is definitely true or correct, especially by providing evidence
words.example: confirm something, His guilty expression confirmed my suspicions.

words.def: to make somebody unable to think clearly or understand something
words.example: confuse somebody, These two sets of statistics are guaranteed to confuse the public.

words.def: unable to think clearly or to understand what is happening or what somebody is saying
words.example: I'm confused—say all that again.

words.def: to join together two or more things; to be joined together
words.example: connect A and B, The towns are connected by train and bus services.

words.def: something that connects two facts, ideas, etc.
words.example: connection between A and B, Scientists have established a connection between cholesterol levels and heart disease.

words.def: a result of something that has happened, especially an unpleasant result
words.example: Remember that actions have consequences.

words.def: to use something, especially fuel, energy or time
words.example: The electricity industry consumes large amounts of fossil fuels.

words.def: a person who buys goods or uses services
words.example: consumer spending/demand

words.def: the act of communicating with somebody, especially regularly
words.example: contact with somebody, I don't have much contact with my uncle.

words.def: if something contains something else, it has that thing inside it or as part of it
words.example: This drink doesn't contain any alcohol.

words.def: a box, bottle, etc. in which something can be stored or transported
words.example: a plastic container

words.def: happening or existing for a period of time without being interrupted
words.example: Recovery after the accident will be a continuous process that may take several months.

words.def: a difference between two or more people or things that you can see clearly when they are compared or put close together; the fact of comparing two or more things in order to show the differences between them
words.example: The two cities make an interesting contrast.

words.def: useful, easy or quick to do; not causing problems
words.example: Fruit is a convenient source of vitamins and energy.

words.def: to make somebody/yourself believe that something is true
words.example: convince somebody/yourself, Are the prime minister's assurances enough to convince the public?

words.def: fairly cold; not hot or warm
words.example: cool air/water

words.def: a plant grown in warm countries for the soft white hairs around its seeds that are used to make cloth and thread
words.example: From the age of six she worked in the fields, picking cotton.

words.def: an act of counting to find the total number of somebody/something
words.example: If the election result is close, there will be a second count.

words.def: land outside towns and cities, with fields, woods, etc.
words.example: The surrounding countryside is windswept and rocky.

words.def: the place where legal trials take place and where crimes, etc. are judged
words.example: the civil/criminal courts

words.def: a thing that is put over or on another thing, usually to protect it or to decorate it
words.example: Brighten up your room with some colourful cushion covers.

words.def: having a layer or amount of something on it
words.example: His face was covered in blood.

words.def: between yellow and white in colour
words.example: a cream linen suit

words.def: having a desire to cause physical or mental pain and make somebody suffer
words.example: He was known to be a cruel dictator.

words.def: connected with the culture of a particular society or group, its customs, beliefs, etc.
words.example: Teachers need to be aware of cultural differences.

words.def: having a lot of curls or a curved shape
words.example: long curly hair

words.def: the system of money that a country uses
words.example: trading in foreign currencies

words.def: at the present time
words.example: The hourly charge is currently £35.

words.def: a piece of cloth that is hung to cover a window
words.example: to draw/pull/close the curtains (= to pull them across the window so they cover it)

words.def: an accepted way of behaving or of doing things in a society or a community
words.example: It's a local custom.

words.def: a wound caused by something sharp
words.example: cuts and bruises on the face

words.def: a bicycle or motorcycle
words.example: He was riding his cycle home when he was hit by a lorry.

words.def: happening, done or produced every day
words.example: Many people still read a daily newspaper.

words.def: physical harm caused to something which makes it less attractive, useful or valuable
words.example: serious/severe damage

words.def: an agreement, especially in business, on particular conditions for buying or doing something
words.example: to sign/strike/finalize/close a deal

words.def: a choice or judgement that you make after thinking and talking about what is the best thing to do
words.example: I think I've made the right decision.

words.def: to say or explain what the meaning of a word or phrase is
words.example: define something, The term ‘normal’ is difficult to define.

words.def: sure or certain; unlikely to change
words.example: Can you give me a definite answer by tomorrow?

words.def: a way of emphasizing that something is true and that there is no doubt about it
words.example: I definitely remember sending the letter.

words.def: an explanation of the meaning of a word or phrase, especially in a dictionary
words.example: The dictionary provides clear, simple definitions.

words.def: to take goods, letters, etc. to the person or people they have been sent to
words.example: deliver something, I get my food delivered from the supermarket to save time.

words.def: a section of a large organization such as a government, business, university, etc.
words.example: department of something, the Department of Health

words.def: an act of leaving a place
words.example: His sudden departure threw the office into chaos.

words.def: used to show that something happened or is true although something else might have happened to prevent it
words.example: Her voice was shaking despite all her efforts to control it.

words.def: a place to which somebody/something is going or being sent
words.example: popular tourist/holiday destinations like the Bahamas

words.def: a person, especially a police officer, whose job is to investigate crimes and catch criminals
words.example: a police/homicide/undercover detective

words.def: to discover the facts about something; to calculate something exactly
words.example: determine something, An inquiry was set up to determine the cause of the accident.
